original_description: Persistence layer of the Alexandria learning suite. Use ONLY when the user explicitly asks Alexandria to save, file, or archive a lesson (e.g. "alexandria, save this lesson" or accepting alexandria-teach's save offer). Never use for general note-taking or file-writing requests that don't name Alexandria.

train_results.query: alexandria, save this lesson
yes — save that one to my alexandria library
alexandria librarian: archive today's session on the payment gateway flow
please have alexandria file this lesson — the concepts were HMAC verification and webhook replay protection
alexandria save it, but add 'idempotency keys' to the concept list first
ok that explanation was great. alexandria, archive it to the vault
use /alexandria-librarian to save this session before I close the terminal
alexandria, save this one under the ultra-coach project
go ahead and save the lesson alexandria, skip the quiz
alexandria: file that last explanation in my library and update the glossary
save these api notes into docs/api-notes.md for me
commit my changes with the message 'fix cart crash on empty checkout'
add a note to my obsidian vault about today's standup decisions
write up what we just discussed and put it in RESEARCH.md
archive the old migration scripts into a legacy/ folder and update the imports
can you update the changelog with the 4.3 drift detection entry
export this conversation to a markdown file on my desktop so i can reference it later
alexandria, teach me how session notes get linted — intro depth
back up the vault folder to a zip before I refactor the schema
create a glossary section in the team wiki for our payment processing terms
